claimText: Fixed-Dose Combination (FDC) drugs are classified as a 'new drug' under the New Drugs and Clinical Trials (NDCT) Rules, 2019, when they combine two or more already approved drugs in a fixed ratio.
evidenceExcerpt: FDCs are considered new drug (under NDCT Rules, 2019) when it combines two or more drugs already approved for individual use in a fixed ratio.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal definition and regulatory status of FDCs in India.
distractorHooks: FDCs are exempt from new drug regulations
FDCs are classified as generic drugs under the Pharmacy Act
FDCs are governed under the Essential Commodities Act

claimText: The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) is the National Regulatory Authority of India operating under the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.
evidenceExcerpt: Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) under Directorate General of Health Services, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare is National Regulatory Authority of India.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying regulatory bodies and their nodal ministries.
distractorHooks: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)

claimText: The National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA) is responsible for enforcing the provisions of the Drugs (Prices Control) Order.
evidenceExcerpt: National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA) enforces provisions of Drugs (Prices Control) Order.
examUse: Match-the-following or statement-based question on pharmaceutical pricing regulation.
distractorHooks: Competition Commission of India regulates drug prices
Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) sets drug prices

claimText: Schedule M of the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules prescribes the Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) for pharmaceutical products in India.
evidenceExcerpt: Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP): Schedule M of Drugs and Cosmetics Rules prescribes Good Manufacturing...
examUse: Correct-pair question matching Schedules of the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules to their respective subjects.

claimText: In India's pharmaceutical regulatory framework, State Drug Regulatory Authorities are responsible for granting licenses and conducting inspections at the state level.
evidenceExcerpt: State Drug Regulatory Authorities grant licenses and conduct inspections at the state level.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing the roles of central (CDSCO) and state drug regulatory authorities.
distractorHooks: CDSCO grants all manufacturing licenses directly
Local municipal bodies grant drug manufacturing licenses
